elementary.title: A2 Have to, don't have to, must, mustn't
elementary.content:
📌 Tổng quan: Nghĩa và cách dùng
ModalNghĩaVí dụ
mustBắt buộc (theo ý kiến người nói)I must stop smoking.
have toBắt buộc (quy tắc bên ngoài)I have to wear a uniform.
mustn'tCấm / không được phépYou mustn't smoke here.
don't have toKhông cần thiết (nhưng được phép)You don't have to come early.
⚠️ QUAN TRỌNG: mustn't ≠ don't have to
Hai cụm này có nghĩa hoàn toàn trái ngược nhau!

• You mustn't wait here. = You can't wait here. (Bị cấm!)
• You don't have to wait here. = You can wait here, but it's not necessary. (Không cần nhưng được phép)

📌 Khi nào dùng must vs have to?
• must → nghĩa vụ xuất phát từ ý kiến người nói: I must study harder.
• have to → nghĩa vụ xuất phát từ quy tắc bên ngoài: We have to wear uniforms.
📐 Cấu trúc ngữ pháp
must / mustn'thave to / don't have to
Khẳng địnhS + must + VS + have/has to + V
Phủ địnhS + mustn't + VS + don't/doesn't have to + V
Câu hỏiMust + S + V?Do/Does + S + have to + V?
Quá khứ❌ Không có dạng quá khứS + had to + V

⚠️ Modal verbs + infinitive (KHÔNG có 'to'):
❌ I must to go. ✅ I must go.
❌ He musts come. ✅ He must come. (không thêm -s)

exercises.q: You ___ wear a seatbelt in a car. It's the law.
exercises.exp: Luật lệ bên ngoài → must/have to

exercises.q: It's a holiday tomorrow. We ___ go to work.
exercises.exp: Không cần (nhưng được phép) → don't have to

exercises.q: You ___ touch that wire! It's dangerous!
___ you have to wear a uniform at your school?
exercises.exp: Have to dùng do/does trong câu hỏi

exercises.q: She ___ take medicine three times a day. The doctor said so.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ bên ngoài (bác sĩ) → has to

exercises.q: This is a library. You ___ be quiet.
exercises.exp: Quy tắc nơi công cộng → must

exercises.q: The entrance is free. You ___ buy a ticket.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết → don't have to

exercises.q: He ___ finish the project by Friday. His boss told him.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ từ sếp → has to

exercises.q: You ___ use your phone during the exam. It's forbidden.
I ___ be at the airport before 6 AM tomorrow.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ thực tế → have to

exercises.q: You look tired. I think you ___ rest.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên / ý kiến người nói → must

exercises.q: ___ I have to fill in this form?
exercises.exp: Have to → dùng do trong câu hỏi

exercises.q: Visitors ___ feed the animals at the zoo. It's not allowed.
We ___ leave yet. The meeting doesn't start until 3.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ call my mum. She'll be worried.
She ___ take her dog to the vet yesterday. He was sick.
exercises.exp: Quá khứ của have to → had to

exercises.q: Children ___ cross the road without looking both ways.
You ___ bring food. There will be plenty at the party.
All passengers ___ show their passport at the border.
We ___ wear shoes inside the house. We always take them off.
You ___ (must/not) park here. It's a no-parking zone.
___ (she/have to) work on Saturdays?
exercises.exp: have to → Does + S + have to

exercises.q: I ___ (have to) wake up early yesterday for my flight.
You ___ (not/have to) bring anything. We have everything we need.
Students ___ (must) submit their assignments on time.
He ___ (not/have to) wear a tie today because it's casual Friday.
exercises.exp: Không bắt buộc → doesn't have to

exercises.q: You ___ (must/not) touch the exhibits in the museum.
___ (I/must) finish this today, or can I do it tomorrow?
She ___ (have to) go to hospital last week. She was very ill.
We ___ (must) try this restaurant. Everyone says it's amazing.
You ___ (must/not) tell anyone about this. It's a secret!
___ (they/have to) bring their own equipment?
exercises.exp: have to câu hỏi → Do + S + have to

exercises.q: I ___ (not/have to) cook tonight. My husband is cooking.
Drivers ___ (must) stop at a red light.
You ___ (must/not) swim here. There are sharks!
She ___ (have to) leave early because of her train.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ thực tế → has to

exercises.q: We ___ (not/have to) pay for parking on Sundays.
You ___ (must) try the local food when you visit Vietnam.
Children under 12 ___ (not/have to) buy a ticket.
He ___ (must/not) forget his passport. The flight is tomorrow.

passage.text: Tom started a new job last week. On his first day, his manager explained all the rules. 'You {{1}} (must) arrive before 9 AM every morning,' she said. 'And you {{2}} (must/not) be late for meetings.' Tom also learnt that he {{3}} (have to) wear a name badge at all times. Fortunately, he {{4}} (not/have to) wear a suit every day – smart casual clothes are fine. At lunchtime, he discovered that he {{5}} (not/have to) eat in the canteen – he could go out if he wanted. However, he {{6}} (must/not) take more than one hour for lunch. After work, Tom felt tired but happy. He {{7}} (have to) learn a lot of new things. He {{8}} (must) remember all the rules.

Anna is planning a camping trip with her friends. 'We {{1}} (have to) book the campsite in advance – it's very popular,' she said. 'And we {{2}} (must/not) forget to bring warm clothes because it gets cold at night.' Her friend Jake said: 'We {{3}} (not/have to) bring food – there's a shop nearby.' But Anna replied: 'Actually, we {{4}} (must) bring water because there's no clean water at the campsite.' Jake asked: '{{5}} (we/have to) bring a tent or do they provide one?' Anna said: 'We {{6}} (must/not) forget the tent – they don't provide any equipment. But we {{7}} (not/have to) bring sleeping bags because I have four of them.'

When I was a student, school life was very strict. We {{1}} (have to) wear a uniform every day. We {{2}} (must/not) bring mobile phones to class. We {{3}} (have to) stand up when the teacher entered the room. Boys {{4}} (have to) have short hair and girls {{5}} (have to) tie their hair back. We {{6}} (not/have to) study on Sundays, which was great. But we {{7}} (have to) do homework every evening. I remember that we {{8}} (must/not) talk during exams. If a student broke the rules, they {{9}} (have to) stay after school. Things are very different now – students {{10}} (not/have to) wear uniforms at my local school.

At the swimming pool, there are many rules. Swimmers {{1}} (must/not) run on the wet floor because it's slippery and dangerous. Children under eight {{2}} (have to) be accompanied by an adult at all times. Swimmers {{3}} (have to) shower before entering the pool. You {{4}} (must/not) bring glass bottles inside the pool area. On the positive side, members {{5}} (not/have to) pay each time they visit because they have a monthly pass. You also {{6}} (not/have to) book a lane in advance – you can just turn up. However, during peak hours, you {{7}} (have to) share a lane with other swimmers. The pool {{8}} (must/not) be used after 10 PM.

Flying for the first time can be confusing. There are many things you {{1}} (must) know before you go. You {{2}} (have to) arrive at the airport at least two hours before your flight. You {{3}} (must/not) bring liquids over 100ml in your hand luggage. However, you {{4}} (not/have to) pay for water after security – most airports have free water fountains. At the gate, you {{5}} (have to) show your boarding pass. During the flight, you {{6}} (must/not) use your mobile phone without switching to flight mode. You {{7}} (not/have to) stay in your seat the whole time, but you {{8}} (must) wear your seatbelt when the sign is on. When you land, you {{9}} (have to) collect your checked luggage at the baggage claim area. Relax – you {{10}} (not/have to) worry too much!

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy mô tả các quy tắc quan trọng tại nơi làm việc hoặc trường học của bạn. Dùng must, mustn't, have to và don't have to để giải thích những gì bắt buộc, bị cấm và không cần thiết.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, mustn't, have to, don't have to
hint_words: don't have to work on weekends
writing_prompts.example: At my workplace, there are several important rules that all employees must follow. We have to arrive before 9 AM every morning and we must always inform our manager if we are going to be late. We mustn't use our personal phones during meetings because it is considered very rude and unprofessional. However, we don't have to wear a formal suit every day – smart casual clothing is perfectly acceptable. We also don't have to stay after 6 PM unless we have a deadline, which I think is a very fair policy.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang chuẩn bị cho một chuyến đi nước ngoài lần đầu. Viết về những gì bạn phải làm, không được làm và không cần làm trước khi đi, trong chuyến đi và khi về.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, mustn't, have to, don't have to
hint_words: don't have to exchange all money
writing_prompts.example: Before my first trip abroad, I had to do many things to prepare properly. I had to apply for a visa one month in advance and I had to book my hotel and flights early because they were very popular. I must not forget my passport on the day of travel – it is absolutely essential. At the airport, I have to arrive at least two hours early and I mustn't bring any liquids over 100ml in my hand luggage. Luckily, I don't have to carry a lot of cash because most places accept credit cards nowadays.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về các quy tắc an toàn trong nhà hoặc nơi công cộng mà bạn muốn chia sẻ với trẻ em. Dùng must, mustn't, have to, don't have to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, mustn't, have to, don't have to
writing_prompts.example: There are some very important safety rules that all children must know and follow every day. When crossing the road, you must always stop and look both ways before taking a single step. You mustn't talk to strangers or accept anything from people you don't know, no matter how friendly they seem. If you get lost in a public place, you have to find a police officer or a shop assistant and ask for help. You don't have to be afraid – there are always kind adults who will help you get back to your parents safely.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy kể về lần bạn phải làm một việc gì đó bắt buộc nhưng không muốn (như đi khám bệnh, học một môn học khó, làm thêm giờ). Dùng had to, must, và mustn't để kể câu chuyện.
writing_prompts.example: Last month, I had to go to hospital because I had a terrible pain in my stomach. The doctor examined me carefully and said I had to stay in hospital for two days for observation. I mustn't eat any solid food for the first twenty-four hours, which was very difficult because I was quite hungry. I had to cancel all my plans for that week and I had to call my boss to explain the situation. Although it was an unpleasant experience, I must admit that the doctors and nurses were extremely kind and professional throughout my stay.

writing_prompts.vi: Tưởng tượng bạn là hướng dẫn viên du lịch. Viết hướng dẫn cho khách tham quan một địa điểm nổi tiếng ở Việt Nam (như Hội An, Vịnh Hạ Long, Phố Cổ Hà Nội). Dùng must, mustn't, have to, don't have to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, mustn't, have to, don't have to
hint_words: don't have to hire a guide
writing_prompts.example: Welcome to Hoi An Ancient Town! There are a few important things you must know before you begin your visit. First, you have to buy an entrance ticket at the main gate – children under five don't have to pay. When visiting the ancient temples and community houses, you must dress modestly and cover your shoulders and knees out of respect for local customs. You mustn't litter anywhere in the old town because Hoi An is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and keeping it clean is very important. You don't have to hire a tour guide – you can explore freely on your own, but a guide can make the experience much richer.

elementary.title: A2 Should, shouldn't
elementary.content:
📌 Should / Shouldn't – Lời khuyên và ý kiến
Dùng should để đưa ra lời khuyên hoặc nói điều gì đó là đúng, là tốt.
Dùng shouldn't để nói điều gì đó là sai, là không nên làm.

Cấu trúcVí dụ
S + should + VYou should rest. You look tired.
S + shouldn't + VHe shouldn't drive so fast.
Should + S + V?Should I see a doctor?

⚠️ Should là modal verb → KHÔNG thêm to, KHÔNG thêm -s:
❌ You should to rest. ✅ You should rest.
❌ He shoulds rest. ✅ He should rest.
💬 Các cách dùng thường gặp
• Đưa ra lời khuyên: You should see a doctor.
• Nói điều đúng/sai về mặt đạo đức: People should be kind to each other.
• Hỏi ý kiến: Do you think I should call him?
• Ý kiến cá nhân (lịch sự hơn): I think you should apologize.
• Thay thế: ought to = should (trang trọng hơn): You ought to rest.
🔄 So sánh mức độ bắt buộc
ModalMức độVí dụ
mustRất mạnh 🔴You must stop smoking. (rất quan trọng)
shouldVừa phải 🟡You should stop smoking. (lời khuyên)

exercises.q: You look pale. You ___ see a doctor.

You ___ drive so fast. It's dangerous.
___ I take an umbrella? It looks like rain.

passage.text: Anna has been feeling stressed and tired for weeks. Her friend Lisa gave her some advice. 'You {{1}} (should) see a doctor,' said Lisa. 'And you {{2}} (shouldn't) work so many hours every day. You {{3}} (should) take regular breaks.' Anna replied: 'I know, but I have so much to do!' Lisa said: 'You {{4}} (should) make a list of your tasks and do them one at a time. You {{5}} (shouldn't) try to do everything at once.' She also said: 'I think you {{6}} (should) go for a walk every evening. Exercise really helps with stress. And you {{7}} (shouldn't) drink so much coffee – it makes you more anxious.' Anna agreed: 'You're right. I {{8}} (should) look after myself better.'

My doctor gave me some health advice last month. She told me I {{1}} (should) eat more fruit and vegetables every day. She said I {{2}} (shouldn't) eat too much red meat or processed food. She recommended that I {{3}} (should) drink at least eight glasses of water daily. She also said I {{4}} (shouldn't) skip breakfast because it gives you energy for the day. My doctor thinks I {{5}} (should) do at least thirty minutes of exercise three times a week. She told me I {{6}} (shouldn't) sit at my desk for more than two hours without a break. Finally, she said I {{7}} (should) go to bed earlier because sleep is very important for health. I think everyone {{8}} (should) follow this kind of advice.

Are you planning to learn a new language? Here is some advice. First, you {{1}} (should) set a clear goal – decide why you want to learn the language. You {{2}} (shouldn't) try to learn too many new words at once because it's overwhelming. You {{3}} (should) practise a little every day rather than for a long time once a week. You {{4}} (shouldn't) be afraid of making mistakes – they help you learn. I think you {{5}} (should) find a language partner to practise speaking with. You {{6}} (should) also watch films and listen to music in the language. You {{7}} (shouldn't) translate every single word – try to understand the general meaning. And you {{8}} (should) be patient with yourself. Learning a language takes time!

Tom is visiting a new city for the first time. His local friend Mai gave him some tips. 'You {{1}} (should) visit the old quarter first – it's the most interesting part of the city,' she said. 'You {{2}} (shouldn't) go there by taxi because traffic is terrible. You {{3}} (should) walk or rent a bicycle instead.' Tom asked: '{{4}} (Should/I) try the street food?' Mai replied: 'Definitely! You {{5}} (should) try the local noodle soup – it's amazing. But you {{6}} (shouldn't) eat at places that look dirty.' She added: 'You {{7}} (should) carry some cash because many small shops don't accept cards. And you {{8}} (shouldn't) forget to take lots of photos – the views are beautiful!'

Good friends are very important in life. I think friends {{1}} (should) always be honest with each other, even when the truth is difficult. They {{2}} (shouldn't) talk behind each other's backs or share secrets. Good friends {{3}} (should) support each other during difficult times. They {{4}} (shouldn't) only be there when things are good. I believe you {{5}} (should) tell your friends when they do something wrong – that's what real friends do. Friends {{6}} (shouldn't) judge each other for their mistakes. Everyone {{7}} (should) feel comfortable being themselves around their friends. You {{8}} (shouldn't) have to pretend to be someone you're not. Finally, I think friends {{9}} (should) make time for each other, no matter how busy life gets. A good friendship {{10}} (should) make both people happier.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn có một người bạn đang gặp vấn đề về sức khỏe vì lối sống không lành mạnh (ngủ muộn, ăn uống xấu, ít vận động). Viết lời khuyên chi tiết cho bạn đó dùng should và shouldn't.
writing_prompts.example: I'm really worried about your health recently, and I think you should make some important changes to your daily routine. First, you shouldn't stay up past midnight every night – you should try to sleep before 11 PM so your body can recover properly. You shouldn't eat fast food or instant noodles every day; instead, you should cook simple healthy meals at home. I also think you should exercise at least three times a week – even a thirty-minute walk is enough to start. You shouldn't sit at your computer for eight hours without a break; you should stand up and move around every hour.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về những lời khuyên bạn muốn dành cho một học sinh mới bắt đầu học tiếng Anh. Dùng should và shouldn't để đưa ra gợi ý học tập hiệu quả.
writing_prompts.example: If you are just starting to learn English, here is some advice that I think will really help you. You should practise a little bit every single day rather than studying for many hours only at the weekend. You shouldn't be afraid of making mistakes – mistakes are a natural and important part of learning any language. I think you should watch English films and listen to English songs to train your ear. You shouldn't only focus on memorising grammar rules; you should also practise speaking as much as possible, even if you feel nervous.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về các nguyên tắc ứng xử mà bạn nghĩ mọi người nên tuân theo trong xã hội hiện đại (trên mạng xã hội, nơi công cộng, trong gia đình). Dùng should và shouldn't.
writing_prompts.example: I believe there are several important rules that everyone in modern society should follow to make the world a kinder place. On social media, people should always think carefully before posting something, because words can hurt others very deeply. We shouldn't spread rumours or share information without checking if it is true first. In public places, everyone should respect those around them – we should give up our seats for the elderly and we shouldn't litter or make too much noise. In the family, children should show gratitude to their parents, and parents should listen to and respect their children's feelings too.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là một chuyên gia dinh dưỡng. Viết lời khuyên chi tiết về chế độ ăn uống lành mạnh cho người Việt Nam dùng should và shouldn't.
writing_prompts.example: As a nutritionist, I strongly believe that Vietnamese people should pay more attention to what they eat every day. You should try to include at least five portions of vegetables and fruit in your daily diet because they are full of vitamins and minerals your body needs. You shouldn't drink too many sugary drinks like soda or sweetened milk tea because they contain a lot of calories and can lead to diabetes. You should always eat breakfast in the morning because it gives your brain and body the energy to start the day well. You shouldn't eat very large meals late at night because your body cannot digest food properly while you sleep.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về những lời khuyên bạn muốn chia sẻ với bản thân 10 năm trước (khi còn nhỏ hơn). Điều gì bạn lẽ ra nên làm và không nên làm? Dùng should và shouldn't.
hint_words: should spend more time with family
shouldn't be afraid to try new things
writing_prompts.example: If I could give advice to my younger self from ten years ago, there are many important things I would say. First, I would say: you should study English much more seriously because it will open so many doors in your future career. You shouldn't waste so much time on meaningless things when you could be learning valuable skills. I would also tell myself: you should spend more quality time with your family and grandparents because you will miss them deeply when they are gone. You shouldn't be afraid to try new things or meet new people – every experience teaches you something valuable about life and about yourself.

elementary.title: A2 Might, might not: Possibility
elementary.content:
📌 Might / Might not – Khả năng có thể xảy ra
Dùng might khi bạn nghĩ điều gì đó có thể đúng hoặc có thể xảy ra nhưng bạn không chắc chắn.

Cấu trúcVí dụNghĩa
S + might + VI might go for a run later.Có thể tôi sẽ chạy bộ.
S + might not + VShe might not come to the party.Có thể cô ấy sẽ không đến.

• might = It's possible that someone will do something
• might not = It's possible that someone won't do something

💡 Maybe ↔ might (thường có nghĩa tương đương)
Maybe I will go. = I might go.
⚠️ Lưu ý quan trọng
• Might là modal verb → KHÔNG thêm to, KHÔNG thêm -s:
❌ She mights come. ✅ She might come.
❌ I might to go. ✅ I might go.

• might dùng cho hiện tại VÀ tương lai:

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang lên kế hoạch cho một chuyến du lịch nhưng còn nhiều điều chưa chắc chắn. Hãy viết về những khả năng khác nhau cho chuyến đi đó, dùng might và might not để diễn đạt sự không chắc chắn.
writing_prompts.example: I am thinking about travelling to Japan next spring, but I haven't made any firm plans yet. I might visit Tokyo and Kyoto, but I might not have enough time to see both cities in one trip. The flights might be quite expensive, so I might not be able to book a hotel in the city centre. I might try staying in a traditional Japanese inn instead, which might be a very special experience. I might not speak any Japanese, but I might download a translation app to help me communicate with local people.

writing_prompts.example: I received a strange phone call this morning from a number I didn't recognise. The caller might be an old friend from university who has changed their number. On the other hand, it might be a wrong number and they might not even know me at all. The call might be about something important, like a job interview or a delivery I'm expecting. Or it might not be serious at all – it might just be someone trying to sell me something. I might call back later to find out, or I might not bother if it was just a marketing call.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về các khả năng cho tương lai của bạn trong 5 năm tới. Dùng might và might not để nói về những điều bạn có thể làm nhưng chưa chắc chắn.

writing_prompts.example: When I think about my life in five years, there are many possibilities that excite me. I might get promoted at my current company if I continue to work hard and develop my skills. On the other hand, I might not stay at the same job – I might decide to start my own small business instead. I might move to another city to find better opportunities, although I might not want to leave my family and close friends behind. I might also go back to university to study something new. I might not be able to plan everything perfectly, but I am excited about what the future might bring.

writing_prompts.vi: Trời sắp mưa và bạn đang lo lắng cho các hoạt động ngoài trời đã lên kế hoạch. Viết về những gì có thể xảy ra và những phương án thay thế, dùng might và might not.
writing_prompts.example: We were planning to have a picnic in the park this Saturday, but the weather forecast does not look good at all. It might rain all morning, which would make an outdoor picnic impossible. However, the forecast might not be accurate – sometimes the weather is much better than predicted. If it does rain, we might have to cancel the picnic and find an indoor place instead. We might go to a café or a restaurant and have lunch there. On the other hand, if the rain is light, we might still go to the park with umbrellas. Whatever happens, we might have a great time anyway because it's the company that matters, not the weather.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn nhận thấy một người bạn cư xử khác thường gần đây (ít nói, không trả lời tin nhắn, có vẻ buồn). Viết về các lý do có thể giải thích hành vi của người bạn đó, dùng might và might not.

He might be upset about something that happened at work or in his personal life. He might have had an argument with someone close to him, or he might not be feeling well physically. On the other hand, he might just be going through a very stressful period and might not want to burden his friends with his problems. He might need some space and time alone to think things through. I might send him a message to let him know I am here if he needs to talk, but I might not push him too hard if he is not ready to open up yet.

elementary.title: A2 May and might: What's the difference?
elementary.content:
📌 May và Might – Giống và khác nhau
Cả may và might đều dùng để diễn đạt khả năng (possibility), nhưng có một số điểm khác biệt quan trọng.

Cấu trúcVí dụ
S + may + V (nguyên mẫu)It may rain tomorrow.
S + may not + VShe may not be home yet.
S + might + V (nguyên mẫu)It might rain tomorrow.
S + might not + VShe might not be home yet.
🔑 Sự khác biệt quan trọng
Tình huốngMay ✅Might ✅
Khả năng tương lai✅ (khả năng cao hơn)✅ (khả năng thấp hơn)
Suy đoán hiện tại✅✅
Xin phép (trang trọng)✅ May I sit here?❌ Không dùng
Cho/từ chối phép✅ You may leave.❌ Không dùng
💡 Thực tế trong giao tiếp
Trong hội thoại hàng ngày, may và might thường có thể dùng thay thế nhau khi nói về khả năng:
✅ It may be too late when we arrive.
✅ It might be too late when we arrive.

Nhưng chỉ dùng may (KHÔNG dùng might) để:
• Xin phép: May I open the window?
• Từ chối phép: You may not use your phone here.
• Cho phép: You may leave when you finish.

⚠️ Không dùng might hoặc may trong câu hỏi về khả năng:
❌ Might it rain? → ✅ Do you think it will rain? / Is it going to rain?

'You {{1}} (may) use the coffee machine in the kitchen whenever you like,' the manager says. Inside, Linda sees a beautiful painting. She thinks: 'This {{6}} (might) be very old – it looks like it's from the Renaissance period.' A gallery assistant approaches and says: 'You {{7}} (may) sit on this bench if you want to study the paintings more closely. It {{8}} (might) take some time to appreciate all the detail.' Linda asks: '{{9}} (May/I) buy a print of this painting in the gift shop?' The assistant smiles: 'You {{10}} (may) – we have several sizes available.'

'The interview {{1}} (might) go well or it {{2}} (might) not – I can't be sure,' she thinks. Her friend gives her advice: 'You {{3}} (might) want to prepare some answers to common questions.' Clara nods: 'They {{4}} (may) ask me about my experience, or they {{5}} (might) focus more on my skills.' Her friend adds: 'You {{6}} (might) need to do a test as well – some companies do that.' Clara is worried: 'What if I don't know the answer to a question?' Her friend replies: 'You {{7}} (may) say you'll look into it – that's perfectly fine.' Clara sighs: 'I {{8}} (might) not get the job, but at least I'll get some useful experience from the interview.' Her friend smiles: 'Don't think negatively! You {{9}} (might) be exactly the person they're looking for. You {{10}} (may) surprise yourself!'

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang xin phép sếp hoặc giáo viên về một số việc (nghỉ sớm, đổi lịch, làm việc từ xa,...). Viết một đoạn hội thoại hoặc email trang trọng, dùng May I để xin phép và may để diễn đạt khả năng.
writing_prompts.example:
Dear Mr. Johnson,
May I take a moment to discuss my schedule for next week? I have a medical appointment on Thursday afternoon, so may I leave the office at 3 PM instead of 6 PM? I will make sure to complete all my urgent tasks before I leave. Alternatively, may I work from home on Friday to make up for the lost time? I may be able to finish the report earlier than expected if I work without distractions at home. I understand this may not be convenient, and I am happy to discuss other arrangements if you prefer. May I come and speak with you about this at a time that suits you?

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về tương lai không chắc chắn của một người nổi tiếng hoặc một nhân vật trong câu chuyện bạn biết. Dùng may và might để đoán các khả năng khác nhau cho tương lai của họ.
writing_prompts.example: The famous football player has been performing poorly this season due to an injury, and many fans are wondering what his future might hold. He may decide to retire from professional football and focus on his family and personal health. On the other hand, he might recover fully and return to the sport stronger than ever. He may pursue a career in coaching young players, sharing all his experience and knowledge. Or he might start a new business venture – he has already shown interest in the sports clothing industry. Whatever he decides, his fans may not see him on the field for much longer, but he might still be a very influential figure in the world of football.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang cân nhắc các lựa chọn cho cuộc sống trong tương lai (thay đổi công việc, chuyển nhà, học thêm bằng cấp). Dùng may và might để diễn đạt những khả năng bạn đang suy nghĩ tới.
hint_words: may apply for a new job
might move to a bigger city
writing_prompts.example: I have been thinking a lot about my future recently, and there are several important decisions I may need to make in the coming months. I might apply for a new job at a different company because I feel I have stopped growing in my current position. I may also consider moving to Ho Chi Minh City, where there might be more opportunities in my field. I am thinking about studying for a professional qualification too – it may help me advance my career significantly. However, I might not make all these changes at once because it could be too stressful. I may start with just one change and see how it goes before making any other big decisions.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về các quy định xin phép tại một nơi công cộng bạn biết (bảo tàng, thư viện, công viên quốc gia, bệnh viện). Dùng may để nói về những điều được phép và may not cho những điều bị cấm.
writing_prompts.example: When visiting a national park in Vietnam, visitors may enjoy many wonderful activities and experiences. You may take photographs anywhere in the park as long as you do not disturb the wildlife or damage the natural environment. You may bring your own food and eat it in designated picnic areas. However, you may not feed wild animals under any circumstances, as this can harm them and disrupt the natural ecosystem. Visitors may not enter restricted zones or protected areas without special permission from the park authorities. You may hire a local guide if you wish to explore deeper into the forest, and this is strongly recommended for safety reasons.

might be about a job offer
may not know I changed number
writing_prompts.example: This morning I received a mysterious phone call from a number I don't recognise. The caller didn't leave a message, so I am now wondering who it might be. It may be from one of my old friends from university who has got a new phone number. Or it might be a recruiter calling about a job application I submitted last week – I have been waiting to hear back from two companies. The caller may not know that I rarely answer unknown numbers. On the other hand, it might have been a wrong number and the person may have already realised their mistake. I may try calling back this evening to find out, although it might just be an automated marketing call, in which case I may not bother.

elementary.title: A2 Used to, didn't use to: Past habits and states
elementary.content:
📌 Used to là gì?
Used to diễn đạt thói quen hoặc trạng thái trong quá khứ mà hiện tại không còn nữa.

LoạiVí dụNghĩa
Thói quen cũI used to play chess every day.Trước hay chơi, giờ không
Trạng thái cũShe used to be very shy.Trước nhút nhát, giờ không
Sở hữu cũWe used to have a big garden.Trước có, giờ không còn
📐 Cấu trúc ngữ pháp
DạngCấu trúcVí dụ
✅ Khẳng địnhS + used to + V(bare)He used to smoke.
❌ Phủ địnhS + didn't use to + V(bare)He didn't use to smoke.
❓ Câu hỏiDid + S + use to + V(bare)?Did you use to smoke?

⚠️ Chú ý chính tả quan trọng: Phủ định & câu hỏi viết use to (không có d)
  ❌ He didn't used to go.  ✅ He didn't use to go.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
1️⃣ Used to KHÔNG có dạng hiện tại → dùng usually / normally + present simple
  ❌ I use to get up early.  ✅ I usually get up early.

2️⃣ Chỉ dùng cho hành động lặp lại hoặc trạng thái, KHÔNG cho sự kiện một lần:
  ❌ I used to go to Paris last year.  ✅ I went to Paris last year.

3️⃣ Used to ≠ be used to + V-ing (quen với việc gì):
  • I used to drive on the left. (Trước đây lái trái, giờ không.)
  • I am used to driving on the left. (Tôi quen lái bên trái.)

writing_prompts.vi: Viết câu mô tả thói quen ăn uống của bạn hồi nhỏ so với bây giờ. Dùng 'used to' và 'didn't use to'. Cho biết ít nhất 3 thứ khác nhau giữa trước và sau.
writing_prompts.example: When I was a child, I used to eat a lot of sweets and junk food every day after school, but I didn't use to eat vegetables at all because I thought they were disgusting. Now I eat salad for lunch and I didn't use to drink coffee, but these days I have two cups every morning to wake up.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả cuộc sống của ông bà hoặc cha mẹ bạn khi họ còn trẻ. Dùng 'used to / didn't use to'. Viết ít nhất 5 câu về thói quen, điều kiện sống và những thứ không tồn tại hồi đó.
writing_prompts.example: My grandparents used to live in a small village without any electricity or running water. They used to wake up at four in the morning to work in the fields. My grandmother used to make all their clothes by hand because they didn't use to have much money to buy new ones. They didn't use to travel far from the village, and they didn't use to have any technology like phones or computers.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn ngắn về một thói quen hoặc sở thích bạn đã bỏ. Giải thích tại sao bạn thay đổi. Dùng 'used to', 'didn't use to', và past simple cho các sự kiện cụ thể.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to, didn't use to, past simple
writing_prompts.example: I used to play video games for hours every night when I was in secondary school, and I didn't use to care much about exercise or going outside. Then one day my doctor told me I needed to be more active, so I joined a running club. Now I run three times a week and I don't even miss gaming that much.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn ngắn về cuộc sống ở thành phố hoặc đất nước bạn 50 năm trước. Dùng 'used to / didn't use to' để mô tả những gì đã thay đổi về giao thông, công nghệ, và thói quen xã hội.
writing_prompts.example: Fifty years ago, people in this city used to travel everywhere by bicycle or on foot because there didn't use to be many cars. Families used to eat dinner together every evening and they didn't use to watch much television. Shops used to close on Sundays and people didn't use to work at weekends. Life used to be slower and quieter, but people didn't use to feel as stressed as they do today.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy tưởng tượng bạn đang phỏng vấn một người lớn tuổi về thời trẻ của họ. Viết 5-6 câu hỏi dùng 'Did you use to...' và sau đó viết câu trả lời ngắn phù hợp cho mỗi câu hỏi.
writing_prompts.example: Did you use to play outside a lot when you were young? — Yes, I did. We used to play in the street every afternoon. Did you use to have a television at home? — No, we didn't use to have one until I was ten. Did you use to go on holidays abroad? — No, we didn't. We didn't use to have enough money for that.

elementary.title: A2 How to use the verb 'go' in English
elementary.content:
📌 Tổng quan: Các cách dùng động từ "go"
Cấu trúcDùng khiVí dụ
go + -ingThể thao, hoạt động giải tríLet's go swimming!
go to + nơi chốnĐến một địa điểmI go to the gym every day.
go to work / bed / schoolKhông dùng the (thành ngữ)She goes to work at 8.
go + home / there / abroadTrạng từ chỉ nơi chốn (không có to)I'm going home now.
go for a + nounCác hoạt động thông thườngShall we go for a walk?
🏃 go + -ing: Thể thao & Hoạt động
Thể thao / Hoạt độngVí dụ
swimming, skiing, cycling, joggingI go cycling on weekends.
shopping, dancing, camping, fishingLet's go shopping this afternoon.
hiking, sightseeing, kayaking, sailingWe went hiking last weekend.
📍 go to: Đến nơi chốn
• go to + the + nơi công cộng: go to the cinema / the park / the beach / the doctor
• go to + nơi KHÔNG có the (thành ngữ cố định):
  go to work / go to school / go to university / go to bed / go to prison

• go + home / abroad / there / here: KHÔNG dùng to trước các trạng từ này
  ❌ I'm going to home.  ✅ I'm going home.
🚶 go for a: Hoạt động thông thường
go for a walk / a run / a drive / a swim / a drink / a meal

⚠️ So sánh: go swimming (thói quen) vs go for a swim (một lần cụ thể)
• I go swimming every Saturday. (thói quen)
• Shall we go for a swim now? (lần này cụ thể)

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả thói quen tập thể dục hoặc hoạt động ngoài trời của bạn. Dùng 'go + -ing', 'go for a', và 'go to the'. Đề cập ít nhất 4 hoạt động khác nhau và tần suất.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: go + -ing, go for a, go to the
writing_prompts.example: I try to stay active throughout the week. I go jogging every morning before breakfast, even when it's cold, because it really wakes me up. On Tuesdays and Thursdays, I go to the gym and do some weight training. At the weekend, I usually go for a long walk in the park with my dog, and if the weather is good, we sometimes go cycling along the river path. Once a month, my friends and I go swimming at the local pool.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một email ngắn cho bạn bè, mời họ tham gia kế hoạch cuối tuần của bạn. Đề xuất ít nhất 3 hoạt động dùng 'go + -ing', 'go for a', 'go to the'. Giải thích tại sao mỗi hoạt động này thú vị.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: go + -ing, go for a, go to the
writing_prompts.example: Hi Lan! I'm planning an amazing weekend and I'd love you to join us. On Saturday morning, shall we go hiking in the forest near the lake? The autumn colours are beautiful right now. In the afternoon, we could go for a meal at that new restaurant by the market — I heard the food is incredible. And on Saturday evening, let's go dancing at the new club downtown — they have live music every weekend. On Sunday, we could go to the cinema to see the new film everyone is talking about. What do you think?

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một kỳ nghỉ lý tưởng, trong đó bạn tham gia nhiều hoạt động khác nhau mỗi ngày. Dùng ít nhất 6 cấu trúc 'go + -ing' hoặc 'go for a' khác nhau. Cho biết địa điểm và cảm xúc.
writing_prompts.example: My ideal holiday would be two weeks in New Zealand. Every morning, I would go jogging along the beach to enjoy the fresh sea air. After breakfast, I'd go kayaking on the beautiful lakes or go hiking in the national parks. In the afternoons, I'd go sightseeing in the towns and visit local markets. Some evenings, we'd go for a meal at a restaurant serving fresh local seafood, and on other evenings, we could go dancing at a local bar. I'd definitely go skiing in the South Island — the mountains there look incredible.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một chuyến đi du lịch đáng nhớ của bạn. Dùng các cấu trúc 'go + -ing', 'go to', 'went', 'going'. Viết ít nhất 5 câu về nơi bạn đã đi, những gì bạn làm, và những gì bạn thích hoặc không thích.
writing_prompts.example: Last summer, I went to Thailand with my family for two weeks. We went to Bangkok first and visited many temples. Every morning, we went swimming at the beautiful beach near our hotel. On some days, we went shopping at the huge markets to buy souvenirs. We also went on a boat tour around the islands. It was an amazing experience and I would definitely recommend going to Thailand for your next holiday.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những hoạt động bạn thường làm vào cuối tuần. Dùng 'go + -ing' để mô tả các hoạt động như đi bơi, đi mua sắm, đi dạo. Giải thích tại sao bạn thích những hoạt động đó.
writing_prompts.example: Every weekend, I usually go swimming at the public pool near my house because I want to stay fit and healthy. On Saturday mornings, I go to the gym for a workout session. Sometimes I go shopping at the mall with my friends to buy new clothes or just to browse around. In the evenings, I often go for a walk in the park with my dog. I really enjoy these weekend activities because they help me relax after a busy week at work.

elementary.title: A2 The different uses of the verb 'get'
elementary.content:
📌 Tổng quan: Các nghĩa chính của "get"
NghĩaCấu trúcVí dụ
📦 Nhận / Lấy / Muaget + nounI got a letter this morning.
🚗 Đến nơi / Về tớiget + to / homeWhat time do you get to work?
🔄 Trở nên (thay đổi)get + adjectiveIt's getting dark outside.
🤝 Cụm động từget + prepositionPlease get on the bus.
⬆️ Di chuyểnget + in/out/up/offGet in the car!
🔄 get + adjective: Trở nên / Thay đổi trạng thái
Ví dụNghĩa
It's getting cold.Trời đang trở nên lạnh.
She got angry.Cô ấy trở nên tức giận.
He got married last year.Anh ấy kết hôn năm ngoái.
I'm getting better.Tôi đang hồi phục dần.
🤝 Phrasal verbs phổ biến với get
CụmNghĩaVí dụ
get upThức dậy / đứng dậyI get up at 7 every day.
get on / offLên / xuống (bus, train)Get on the bus at stop 5.
get in / out ofVào / ra khỏi (xe hơi)Get in the car, we're late!
get along (with)Hòa thuận vớiI get along well with my sister.
get backTrở vềWe got back from holiday yesterday.
get rid ofLoại bỏ / tống khứI want to get rid of this old sofa.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả buổi sáng điển hình của bạn từ lúc thức dậy đến khi đến nơi làm việc hoặc trường học. Dùng ít nhất 5 cụm có 'get': get up, get dressed, get to, get on/off, get + adjective.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get up, get dressed, get to, get on/off, get + adjective
writing_prompts.example: My mornings are always quite rushed. I usually get up at six thirty, but sometimes I sleep through my alarm and get up late, which makes me stressed. I get dressed quickly and grab some breakfast before leaving the house. I get on the bus at the stop near my building and ride for about twenty minutes. When I finally get to school, I'm usually a bit tired but also excited for the day. If I'm running late, I get very anxious on the bus.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn về lần bạn bị ốm và quá trình hồi phục. Dùng 'get ill', 'get better', 'get worse', 'get to the doctor', và ít nhất 2 cụm 'get' khác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get ill, get cold, get better, get worse, get to, get + adjective
writing_prompts.example: Last winter, I got very ill with a bad flu. It started after I got caught in the rain without a jacket — I got cold and wet, and by the evening I had a terrible fever. I got worse during the night, so the next morning my mum took me to the doctor. We got to the clinic by taxi because I was too weak to walk. The doctor told me to rest and take medicine. After two days in bed, I started getting better slowly. By the end of the week, I had completely recovered and was getting my energy back.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về mối quan hệ giữa bạn và một người quan trọng trong cuộc sống của bạn. Dùng 'get along', 'get to know', 'get upset', 'get angry', và ít nhất 2 cụm 'get' khác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get along, get to know, get upset, get angry, get + adjective
writing_prompts.example: My best friend and I have known each other for ten years. When we first met, it took a while to get to know each other properly because we were both quite shy. But once we started talking, we realised we got along perfectly — we have the same sense of humour and the same interests. Of course, we sometimes get annoyed with each other when we disagree, and occasionally one of us gets upset about something. But we always talk it through. I got a message from her this morning and it made me smile. I'm really glad we got to be such close friends.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một lần bạn gặp khó khăn và cách bạn vượt qua. Dùng các cấu trúc với 'get': get used to, get over, get better at, get in trouble, get along. Viết ít nhất 5 câu về tình huống và cách bạn xử lý.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get used to, get over, get better at, get in trouble, get along with
writing_prompts.example: When I first started university, I got in trouble for being late to my first important lecture. At first, I couldn't get used to the new schedule and the heavy workload. It took me a few months to get better at managing my time effectively. My classmates helped me get over my initial difficulties. Now I get along very well with everyone in my class, and I can handle my studies much better than before.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một kỹ năng mới mà bạn muốn học (như nấu ăn, lái xe, ngôn ngữ). Giải thích tại sao bạn muốn học nó, kế hoạch học, và những gì bạn hy vọng đạt được. Dùng 'get better at', 'get used to', 'get into'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get better at, get used to, get into
writing_prompts.example: I want to get better at cooking because I eat too much fast food and it is not healthy. I plan to take cooking classes twice a week starting next month. At first, it will be hard to get used to the busy schedule, but I am determined to succeed. I hope to get into cooking more exotic dishes once I master the basics. My mother offered to teach me her special recipes, which would be great because I want to preserve our family traditions.

elementary.title: A2 'Do' vs 'Make': What's the difference?
elementary.content:
📌 Tổng quan: Do vs Make – Khi nào dùng cái nào?
Cả do và make đều có thể dịch là "làm" trong tiếng Việt, nhưng cách dùng rất khác nhau.

DOMAKE
Hoạt động, việc làm, nhiệm vụTạo ra / sản xuất một thứ gì đó
do homework / do sport / do workmake a cake / make dinner / make a mistake
✅ DO: Các cụm thông dụng
CụmNghĩa
do homeworkLàm bài tập về nhà
do sport / exerciseTập thể dục / thể thao
do the houseworkLàm việc nhà
do the dishes / washingRửa bát / giặt quần áo
do a course / examHọc khóa học / thi
do your bestCố gắng hết sức
do someone a favourGiúp ai một việc
✅ MAKE: Các cụm thông dụng
CụmNghĩa
make a mistakeMắc lỗi
make a decisionĐưa ra quyết định
make dinner / lunch / coffeeNấu ăn / pha cà phê
make a phone callGọi điện
make a noise / messGây ồn ào / bừa bộn
make friendsKết bạn
make a plan / suggestionLập kế hoạch / đề xuất

⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ I made my homework.  ✅ I did my homework.
❌ She did a mistake.  ✅ She made a mistake.
❌ He did dinner.  ✅ He made dinner.
❌ They made sport.  ✅ They did sport.

💡 Mẹo nhớ: MAKE thường dùng khi có KẾT QUẢ hoặc SẢN PHẨM tạo ra.
  make a cake ✅ (có chiếc bánh) / make a decision ✅ (có quyết định)
  DO thường dùng cho HOẠT ĐỘNG hoặc NHIỆM VỤ không tạo ra vật thể cụ thể.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả công việc nhà bạn và các thành viên trong gia đình thường làm hàng tuần. Dùng ít nhất 6 cụm khác nhau với 'do' và 'make'. Nói ai làm gì và vào thời gian nào.
writing_prompts.example: In our family, everyone helps with the housework. My mum usually makes breakfast for everyone in the morning and does the shopping on Saturdays. My dad does the dishes after dinner and makes dinner on Sundays. My sister and I do the washing up after school. We take turns doing the laundry and making the beds. My dad sometimes makes mistakes when he cooks, but we all laugh about it. We try to do our best to keep the house clean and tidy.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một ngày học tập hoặc làm việc bận rộn. Dùng ít nhất 5 cụm với 'do' và 3 cụm với 'make'. Mô tả những gì bạn hoàn thành, những lỗi bạn mắc và quyết định bạn đưa ra.
writing_prompts.example: Yesterday was a very busy day at university. I had to do three hours of homework in the morning and then do an important exam in the afternoon. I made a few mistakes on the grammar section, but I think I did well overall. Before the exam, I made a decision not to panic and just do my best. After the exam, my friends and I made a plan to go out for dinner to celebrate. One friend made a suggestion to try a new Vietnamese restaurant, and it was excellent. I came home exhausted but satisfied.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn về lần bạn học nấu ăn một món mới. Dùng 'make' để nói về nấu ăn và 'do' cho các nhiệm vụ khác. Mô tả quá trình, những sai lầm và kết quả.
writing_prompts.example: Last month, I decided to make a traditional Vietnamese pho for the first time. First, I had to do some research online to find the right recipe. I made a shopping list and went to the market. I made a few mistakes — I added too much salt and I didn't do the preparation correctly. The broth didn't taste quite right, so I made a decision to add more spices. After three hours of cooking, the pho was finally ready. It made my whole family very happy. My mum said I should do a cooking course to improve my skills!

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả những gì bạn thường làm (do) và tạo ra (make) trong một ngày bình thường. Phân biệt rõ giữa 'do' và 'make'. Viết ít nhất 5 câu về các hoạt động như làm bài tập, nấu ăn, làm việc.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: do, make, does, makes, did, made
writing_prompts.example: Every morning, I make breakfast for my family before they wake up. Then I do the washing up quickly and get ready for work. During the day, I do my job at the office where I make important decisions about projects. In the evening, I do my English homework to improve my language skills. On weekends, I make time to visit my friends and family. I always try to do my best in everything I do and make sure I keep my promises to people I care about.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một lần bạn mắc lỗi (mistake) hoặc đưa ra quyết định sai. Giải thích bạn đã làm gì sai, tại sao, và bạn đã học được gì từ kinh nghiệm đó. Dùng 'make a mistake', 'make a decision', 'do something wrong'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: make a mistake, make a decision, do something wrong, make progress, make a promise
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I made a big mistake by making a decision to quit my job without having another one lined up. I did something really risky because I was unhappy with my work environment. At first, I thought I made progress by leaving, but then I realised I made things worse for my family. It took me three months to find a new job and we struggled financially. I learned my lesson and now I always think carefully before I make any important decisions in the future.

elementary.title: A2 Verbs with two objects
elementary.content:
📌 Direct object và Indirect object là gì?
Direct object (tân ngữ trực tiếp) trả lời câu hỏi "what?" (cái gì?) hoặc "who?" (ai?)
Indirect object (tân ngữ gián tiếp) trả lời câu hỏi "to whom?" (cho ai?) hoặc "for whom?" (vì ai?)

Một số động từ có thể có hai tân ngữ cùng lúc (một direct + một indirect).
Các động từ thường gặp: bring, buy, cook, find, get, give, lend, make, offer, pass, promise, read, sell, send, show, tell, write
📐 Hai cấu trúc chính
Cấu trúcVí dụ
S + V + indirect obj + direct obj
(không dùng to/for)		She gave me a book.
He told us the truth.
Can you send me the report?
S + V + direct obj + to/for + indirect objShe gave a book to me.
He told the truth to us.
I cooked dinner for my mum.

⚠️ Khi indirect object là đại từ nhân xưng (me, him, her, us, them), thường đặt ngay sau động từ:
✅ She sent me a postcard.    ❌ She sent a postcard to me. (không sai nhưng ít tự nhiên hơn)
🔑 to hay for? — Phụ thuộc vào động từ!
Dùng TODùng FOR
give, lend, offer, pass, promise, read, sell, send, show, tell, writebring, buy, cook, find, get, make
I sent a card to Alex.
She showed the photo to us.		He bought flowers for her.
Can you make coffee for me?

📌 Ghi nhớ nhanh: TO = hướng đến ai đó | FOR = làm lợi cho ai đó
❌ I send to Alex a postcard. ✅ I sent Alex a postcard. / I sent a postcard to Alex.

exercises.exp: tell + indirect object trực tiếp (đại từ)

exercises.exp: write + indirect object trực tiếp (đại từ)

exercises.opts: I gave to her a present.,I gave her a present.,I gave for her a present.,I gave present her.
exercises.exp: Cấu trúc: V + indirect object + direct object (không to/for)

exercises.q: Which sentence is CORRECT?
exercises.opts: She sent a card for Tom.,She sent a card to Tom.,She sent to Tom a card.,She sent Tom to a card.
exercises.exp: send dùng TO, và indirect object sau direct object khi dùng to

exercises.q: My grandmother often ___ me old family stories.
exercises.exp: tell somebody something — tell nhận indirect object trực tiếp

exercises.q: Can you bring ___ some water?
exercises.exp: Đại từ làm indirect object đứng ngay sau động từ

exercises.q: He offered ___ a seat on the crowded bus.
exercises.exp: offer + indirect object trực tiếp (đại từ)

exercises.q: She ___ (give) me a lovely birthday card last year.
exercises.exp: give + indirect object trực tiếp; thì quá khứ: gave

exercises.q: Please pass the bread ___ your father.
exercises.exp: pass dùng TO khi indirect object đứng sau

exercises.q: I bought some chocolates ___ my teacher.
Can you lend me your umbrella? I'll ___ it back tomorrow.
exercises.exp: give back = trả lại, cần indirect object me đứng sau

exercises.q: The manager showed the new employee ___ the office.
exercises.exp: show someone round = chỉ cho ai đó tham quan

exercises.q: He ___ (send) his parents a postcard from Paris.
exercises.exp: send + indirect + direct; quá khứ: sent

exercises.q: My aunt ___ (make) us a delicious cake for the party.
exercises.exp: make + indirect object + direct object; quá khứ: made

exercises.q: Could you read ___ that sign? I forgot my glasses.
I'll write ___ an email when I arrive.
They sold their house ___ a young couple.
The teacher offered ___ (we) some extra help after class.
exercises.exp: offer + us (đại từ tân ngữ) + some extra help

exercises.q: She promised ___ (he) a special dinner if he passed the exam.
exercises.exp: promise + him (đại từ tân ngữ) + a special dinner

exercises.q: Can you get some juice ___ the children?
My friend ___ (lend) me her car for the weekend.
exercises.exp: lend + indirect + direct; quá khứ bất quy tắc: lent

exercises.q: He told ___ (we) an amazing story about his travels.
exercises.exp: tell + us (đại từ tân ngữ) + an amazing story

exercises.q: Please bring ___ (I) a glass of water.
exercises.exp: bring + me (đại từ tân ngữ) + a glass of water

exercises.q: She showed the painting ___ her art teacher.
exercises.exp: show dùng TO + indirect object khi đặt sau direct object

exercises.q: I'll find ___ (you) a good hotel near the city centre.
Dad cooked a big breakfast ___ the whole family.
She ___ (offer) the guests some tea and biscuits.
exercises.exp: offer + indirect object trực tiếp; quá khứ: offered

passage.text: Last weekend, Tom decided to do something special for his family. First, he {{1}} (make) his parents a big breakfast in bed. He {{2}} (bring) them fresh orange juice and toast. His father {{7}} (pass) Tom the bread and {{8}} (offer) him some more soup. At the end of the meal, Tom {{9}} (promise) his parents that he would visit more often. He {{10}} (write) himself a note so he wouldn't forget.

Anna is a very generous person. She always {{1}} (give) her friends advice when they have problems. Last month, she {{2}} (lend) her colleague fifty euros because he forgot his wallet. She also {{3}} (buy) a birthday cake for the whole team at work. When her neighbour was sick, Anna {{4}} (cook) her some soup and {{5}} (bring) it to her house. She {{6}} (read) her some magazines to help her feel better. Anna {{7}} (send) her friend Maria a long letter last week because Maria moved to another city. She {{8}} (write) all the news from their neighbourhood. Anna's mother always says she should {{9}} (find) herself a good job in a caring profession. Anna just laughs and {{10}} (tell) her that she simply loves helping people.

Sophie's aunt {{3}} (make) them a beautiful quilt by hand. Their best friend Tom {{4}} (send) them a card from Australia where he was travelling. He also {{5}} (promise) them a visit when he returned. At the wedding, Jake {{6}} (read) Sophie a poem he had written himself. His words {{7}} (bring) tears to many guests' eyes. Sophie {{8}} (give) Jake a watch that had belonged to her grandfather. After the ceremony, the couple {{9}} (show) everyone their wedding album. Jake's father {{10}} (offer) all the guests a glass of champagne and proposed a toast.

Mr. Green is a popular English teacher. He always {{1}} (tell) his students interesting stories to help them learn. He {{2}} (give) them a lot of encouragement and never makes them feel bad about mistakes. Last week, he {{3}} (bring) the class some English magazines and {{4}} (show) them how to read for enjoyment. He {{5}} (read) them a short story by a famous author. Then he {{6}} (give) each student a different article to read. He {{7}} (lend) some students his own books because they didn't have enough. After class, he {{8}} (write) each student a short personal note about their progress. He {{9}} (offer) the weakest student some extra lessons. He also {{10}} (promise) the whole class a class trip if everyone passed the end-of-term exam.

Clara loves to cook for people. Every Sunday, she {{1}} (make) her flatmates a big lunch. She always {{2}} (find) them new and exciting recipes online. Last Sunday, she {{3}} (cook) everyone a Thai curry. She {{4}} (bring) her neighbour Mrs. Ellis a portion because she lives alone. Clara also {{5}} (give) Mrs. Ellis some vegetables from her garden. In return, Mrs. Ellis {{6}} (read) Clara some poems from an old book. She {{7}} (lend) her the book so she could read more at home. Clara {{8}} (show) her flatmates some photos of the dishes she had made. One flatmate {{9}} (offer) to help wash up. Another {{10}} (promise) to cook next Sunday. They all agreed it was the perfect way to spend the weekend.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một dịp đặc biệt (sinh nhật, lễ Tết, lễ tốt nghiệp...) khi bạn và gia đình/bạn bè trao quà cho nhau. Dùng ít nhất 6 động từ có hai tân ngữ (give, send, make, buy, show, tell...) và mô tả chi tiết ai đã tặng gì cho ai.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: verbs with two objects: give, send, make, buy, show, tell, bring, read, lend
writing_prompts.example: Last year, my family celebrated my grandmother's 70th birthday with a big party. My parents gave her a beautiful gold bracelet that she had always wanted. My brother and I made her a handmade photo album and showed her all the old family pictures inside. My aunt brought everyone delicious homemade cakes and offered the guests some tea. My grandmother read us a poem that she had written herself, which made everyone emotional. After the party, we sent all the relatives some photos by email. It was a wonderful day that I will never forget.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một người bạn bè hoặc thành viên gia đình rất tốt bụng và hào phóng của bạn. Mô tả những điều họ đã làm cho người khác, sử dụng các động từ như give, buy, cook, lend, show, tell, make, bring để miêu tả hành động của họ.
writing_prompts.example: My best friend Linh is one of the most generous people I know. She always gives her friends advice and support whenever they need it. Last year, when I was studying for my final exams, she cooked me healthy meals every evening so I didn't have to worry about food. She also lent me her notes and showed me the best way to study each subject. When I passed, she bought me a lovely notebook as a gift and wrote me a personal message inside it. She always brings us little treats when she visits and tells us funny stories that make everyone laugh.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn vừa trở về sau một chuyến đi du lịch dài ngày. Viết về những món quà lưu niệm bạn mua cho người thân và bạn bè. Mô tả bạn đã tặng gì cho ai, mua gì cho ai và phản ứng của họ khi nhận quà.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: verbs with two objects: gave, bought, brought, sent, showed, made
writing_prompts.example: I just returned from a two-week trip to Japan, and I brought everyone lots of souvenirs. I gave my mother a beautiful silk scarf that she absolutely loved. For my brother, I bought some Japanese sweets and a small model of Mount Fuji. I sent my best friend a postcard from Kyoto every few days while I was away. When I got home, I showed my family hundreds of photos on my laptop and told them all about my adventures. My grandmother asked me to make her some Japanese tea, so I bought her a special tea set and showed her how to use it properly.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một thầy/cô giáo hoặc người cố vấn tuyệt vời trong cuộc đời bạn. Dùng các động từ có hai tân ngữ để mô tả những gì họ đã dạy, chia sẻ, và trao cho bạn và các học sinh/người khác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: verbs with two objects: gave, told, showed, read, lent, offered, taught
writing_prompts.example: My English teacher Mrs. Pham was the most inspiring teacher I ever had. She gave her students a great love of reading and writing from a very young age. She often read us amazing stories during class that made us want to read more. She lent me many books from her personal library when she noticed how much I enjoyed them. She showed me how to express my ideas clearly in writing and told me I had real talent. When I struggled with grammar, she offered me extra lessons after school for free. She wrote me a wonderful reference letter when I applied to university and promised to help me reach my goals.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một truyền thống đặc biệt trong gia đình hoặc cộng đồng của bạn, trong đó mọi người thường trao đổi quà tặng, nấu ăn cho nhau hoặc chia sẻ những điều có ý nghĩa. Sử dụng nhiều động từ có hai tân ngữ.
writing_prompts.example: In my family, we have a wonderful tradition every Lunar New Year. The elders always give the younger family members red envelopes with lucky money inside. My grandmother cooks the whole family a special feast with many traditional dishes. She makes us a big pot of bánh chưng, which takes hours to prepare. My parents tell us children stories about the history of Tết and what it means to our family. We show each other respect by offering our grandparents gifts and flowers. My aunts and uncles bring their children toys and sweets. At the end of the celebration, my grandfather reads us a poem that his father wrote many years ago. It is a beautiful tradition that brings our family closer together.

elementary.title: A2 Stative vs dynamic verbs (or non-action vs action verbs)
elementary.content:
📌 Stative verbs (Động từ trạng thái) là gì?
Stative verbs (hay non-action verbs) mô tả một trạng thái hoặc điều kiện, không phải một hành động. Vì trạng thái không có điểm bắt đầu và kết thúc rõ ràng, các động từ này KHÔNG được dùng với thì tiếp diễn.

❌ I am knowing the answer.    ✅ I know the answer.
❌ She is liking vegetables.    ✅ She likes vegetables.
📋 Các nhóm Stative Verbs phổ biến
NhómCác động từVí dụ
Sở hữubelong, own, have (= possess), possess, containAli owns three cars.
This bag belongs to me.
Suy nghĩ / Ý kiếnknow, believe, agree, think (= believe), understand, imagine, rememberI know the answer.
She believes in herself.
Cảm xúc / Mong muốnlike, love, hate, want, need, wish, prefer, dislikeSam likes vegetables.
They need more time.
Trông có vẻ / Xuất hiệnseem, appear, look (= seem), sound (= seem), taste, smell, feelShe looks tired.
This soup tastes great.
⚡ Dynamic verbs (Động từ hành động) là gì?
Dynamic verbs (hay action verbs) mô tả hành động, sự thay đổi hoặc quá trình có điểm bắt đầu và kết thúc. Chúng có thể dùng với tất cả các thì, kể cả thì tiếp diễn.

Ví dụ: eat, run, swim, speak, walk, sleep, cook, come, go, buy, watch, play, write, read, drive...

✅ I am eating breakfast now. (đang xảy ra)
✅ I eat breakfast at 7 AM every day. (thói quen)
⚠️ Động từ vừa Stative vừa Dynamic — CẨN THẬN!
Động từStative (simple tense)Dynamic (continuous tense)
haveI have two cats. (= sở hữu)They are having a party. (= tổ chức)
thinkI think it's a good idea. (= ý kiến)Sara is thinking about her future. (= suy nghĩ)
beHe is kind. (= tính cách)He is being kind today. (= đang xử sự)
lookShe looks happy. (= trông có vẻ)She is looking out the window. (= nhìn)
tasteThis tastes horrible. (= có vị)I am tasting the soup. (= đang nếm)

exercises.q: I ___ your new hairstyle. It looks great!
exercises.exp: love = cảm xúc → stative verb, không dùng tiếp diễn

exercises.q: She ___ in the garden right now.
exercises.exp: work = dynamic verb, đang diễn ra → present continuous

exercises.q: This pizza ___ amazing! What's in it?
exercises.exp: taste (= có vị) → stative, dùng simple tense

exercises.q: He ___ what to do next. He has no idea.
exercises.exp: know = stative verb, phủ định: doesn't know

exercises.q: We ___ to move to a bigger house soon.
exercises.exp: want = stative verb (mong muốn), không dùng tiếp diễn

exercises.q: Look! The children ___ in the park.
exercises.exp: play = dynamic verb; 'Look!' = dấu hiệu hành động đang diễn ra

exercises.q: This book ___ to my sister. Please don't take it.
exercises.exp: belong = stative (sở hữu), không dùng tiếp diễn

exercises.q: I ___ you're right about that.
She ___ a book at the moment. Please be quiet.
exercises.exp: read = dynamic verb; at the moment = đang diễn ra

exercises.q: He ___ three languages: English, French and Spanish.
exercises.exp: speak (= có khả năng nói) ở đây là stative; thói quen/sự thật

exercises.q: The soup ___ of garlic and herbs. It's delicious!
exercises.exp: smell (= có mùi) → stative, dùng simple tense

exercises.q: They ___ a party right now. You can hear the music!
exercises.exp: have (= tổ chức) = dynamic, đang diễn ra → continuous

exercises.q: I ___ a headache. Can I sit down?
exercises.exp: have (= sở hữu/trạng thái cơ thể) → stative

exercises.q: She ___ very rude to everyone today! What's wrong with her?
exercises.exp: be + adjective (tạm thời, cố ý) → dynamic: is being rude

exercises.q: Which sentence is CORRECT?
exercises.opts: I am knowing the answer.,I know the answer.,I am know the answer.,I knowing the answer.
exercises.exp: know = stative verb → chỉ dùng simple tense

exercises.q: She ___ to music every morning before work.
exercises.exp: every morning = thói quen → present simple với dynamic verb

exercises.q: The children ___ too much noise right now!
exercises.exp: make (noise) = dynamic; right now = đang diễn ra

exercises.q: I ___ coffee, but I prefer tea.
exercises.opts: don't like,am not liking,not like,isn't liking
exercises.exp: like = stative → phủ định: don't like (không dùng tiếp diễn)

exercises.q: He ___ about his future at the moment.
exercises.exp: think (= đang suy nghĩ/quá trình) → dynamic, continuous

exercises.q: This coat ___ very soft. I want to buy it.
exercises.exp: feel (= có cảm giác khi chạm) → stative, simple tense

exercises.q: She ___ (love) animals, especially cats and dogs.
exercises.exp: love = stative verb (cảm xúc), không dùng continuous

exercises.q: We ___ (have) dinner at the moment. Can I call you back?
This milk ___ (smell) bad. I think it's gone off.
exercises.exp: smell (= có mùi) → stative, simple tense

exercises.q: I ___ (not/understand) this grammar rule. Can you explain?
exercises.exp: understand = stative → phủ định simple tense

exercises.q: Look! Tom ___ (run) in the park with his dog.
exercises.exp: run = dynamic; Look! = đang diễn ra → present continuous

exercises.q: She ___ (think) about her holiday plans right now.
exercises.exp: think (= đang suy nghĩ/kế hoạch) = dynamic → continuous

exercises.q: He ___ (own) a small café in the city centre.
The students ___ (work) on a group project this week.
exercises.exp: work = dynamic; this week = đang trong quá trình

exercises.q: This chocolate ___ (taste) absolutely wonderful!
exercises.exp: taste (= có vị) → stative, simple tense

exercises.q: I ___ (need) some help with this exercise.
Why ___ (he/be) so rude to everyone today?
exercises.exp: be + adjective (cố ý, tạm thời) = dynamic → continuous

exercises.q: We ___ (believe) that exercise is important for good health.
She ___ (cook) a big meal for her family right now.
exercises.exp: cook = dynamic; right now = đang diễn ra → continuous

exercises.q: This house ___ (belong) to my grandparents.
The baby ___ (sleep) at the moment, so please be quiet.
exercises.exp: sleep = dynamic; at the moment = đang diễn ra

exercises.q: I ___ (remember) his face but not his name.
She ___ (look) out of the window and watching the rain.
exercises.exp: look (= nhìn, hành động cố ý) = dynamic → continuous

exercises.q: They ___ (want) to visit Japan next year.
He ___ (read) a very interesting article about space travel.
exercises.exp: read = dynamic; hành động đang xảy ra → continuous

exercises.q: I ___ (agree) with everything you've said.

reorder.words: I know the answer to this question
She loves her new job in the city
They are playing football in the park right now
This soup tastes absolutely wonderful today
He doesn't understand the instructions very well
We are having a great time at the party
The bag belongs to my sister not to me
She is cooking dinner for the whole family
I think this is a really good idea
Look the children are running in the garden
He owns a beautiful house near the beach
She is being very kind to everyone today
I need some help with this difficult exercise
This coffee smells amazing what brand is it
They are watching a film at the moment
I believe that hard work always pays off
She is thinking about changing her job
He wants to travel around the world someday
The students are working on their projects this week
I don't like cold weather at all

passage.text: Sarah {{1}} (love) her new life in the city. Every morning, she {{2}} (wake up) early and {{3}} (walk) to a nearby café. She {{4}} (usually/have) a coffee and a croissant for breakfast. Right now, she {{5}} (sit) at her favourite table and {{6}} (read) the newspaper. She {{7}} (enjoy) the quiet morning before work. She {{8}} (work) at a language school and {{9}} (think) it is the perfect job for her. At the moment, her students {{10}} (practise) their English conversation skills. Sarah {{11}} (believe) that speaking regularly is the best way to learn. She {{12}} (not/want) to change her life at all.

Marco {{1}} (be) a chef at a restaurant in Rome. He {{2}} (love) cooking and {{3}} (know) hundreds of recipes by heart. Today is especially busy. Marco {{4}} (prepare) a special menu for a large group. His assistant {{5}} (chop) vegetables while Marco {{6}} (make) the sauce. The restaurant {{7}} (smell) incredible. The manager {{8}} (think) the evening will be very successful. Some guests have already arrived. They {{9}} (sit) at their tables and {{10}} (look) at the menu. One guest {{11}} (not/understand) a dish, so the waiter {{12}} (explain) it to him.

Julia {{1}} (have) a cat called Mimi. She {{2}} (believe) Mimi is the most beautiful cat in the world. Right now, Mimi {{3}} (sleep) on the sofa. Julia {{4}} (work) at her desk nearby. She {{5}} (write) a report for her boss. She {{6}} (not/like) doing paperwork, but she {{7}} (understand) it is necessary. Outside, it {{8}} (rain) heavily. Julia {{9}} (prefer) sunny days. She {{10}} (look forward to) the summer holiday. Her friend Tom {{11}} (call) her right now – she can see his name on the phone screen. She {{12}} (want) to answer, but she needs to finish the report first.

At the market, there is always a lot of activity. The sellers {{1}} (shout) and trying to attract customers. The fruit {{2}} (smell) sweet and fresh. An old lady {{3}} (choose) some mangoes carefully. She {{4}} (want) only the ripest ones. A young man {{5}} (sell) fresh juice and {{6}} (offer) everyone a free sample. The juice {{7}} (taste) delicious. A group of tourists {{8}} (take) photos of the colourful stalls. They {{9}} (not/understand) the prices in the local currency, so a friendly seller {{10}} (help) them. A child {{11}} (run) between the stalls, laughing. His mother {{12}} (not/know) where he is.
